name: Editor
classification_notes: The typeface is a variable grotesque sans-serif with a wide range of weights from Thin to Black and an extensive set of OpenType alternates, consistent with a contemporary grotesque family.
summary: A variable grotesque with eight weights and an unusually deep set of OpenType stylistic alternates for fine-grained character-level control.
description: A variable grotesque spanning Thin to Black with a single-axis weight range and an interactive web editor for live preview. The OpenType feature set is notably broad, offering single and double storey 'a', alternate 'y', 'g', '&' and Greek phi, alongside contextual alternates, spaghetti alternates and headline letter variants. Greek character support extends the language coverage beyond standard Latin.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen Fonts present Editor as a variable grotesque family with eight named weights and a rich set of OpenType features, including stylistic sets for alternate letterforms, ligatures, contextual alternates and case-sensitive punctuation, previewed through an interactive web editor.

name: Ease
classification_notes: Ease presents clean, open letterforms consistent with geometric sans-serif conventions, with a friendly character appropriate to Studio Feixen's graphic design practice.
summary: A five-weight geometric sans by Felix Pfäffli and Robin Eberwein of Studio Feixen, running from Regular to Black.
description: Designed by Felix Pfäffli and Robin Eberwein, Ease sits within Studio Feixen's small in-house type library alongside Noi Grotesk and Studio Feixen Sans. Five weights from Regular to Black cover the range from running text to heavy display use, with consistent geometric proportions throughout. The family has no italic or condensed cuts, keeping it focused and uncluttered.
foundry_blurb: Ease is part of the Studio Feixen Fonts library, designed by Felix Pfäffli and Robin Eberwein and available in five weights: Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold and Black.

name: Noi Grotesk
classification_notes: A Swiss grotesque with humanist warmth, offering extensive OpenType alternates and variable font axes. The foundry frames it as sitting between Swiss tradition and friendliness.
summary: A Swiss grotesque with eight weights, a variable axis and an unusually rich set of OpenType alternates covering Latin, Cyrillic and Greek.
description: Designed by Robin Eberwein and Felix Pfäffli at Studio Feixen, Noi Grotesk 2.0 spans eight weights from Thin to Black with italics throughout, and ships in two variable configurations: one optimised for print and text, another for web and animation. The family carries over 1,500 glyphs and supports 281 languages across Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Modern Greek. Eleven stylistic sets cover everything from single-storey 'a' variants and creamy alternate forms to spaghetti letters and square dots, making it a grotesk with genuine typographic depth beyond its clean Swiss structure.
foundry_blurb: Studio Feixen describe Noi Grotesk as rooted in Swiss tradition with a sense of friendliness, following the principle that form follows beauty. Version 2.0 introduces a variable font in two flavours and expands the feature set with contextual alternates, discretionary ligatures and a broad language coverage of 281 languages.
